subject: Latest Sherrod news for Peter -- MSNBC and NYT
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Sherrod Brown
body:
|
---

| | Did you happen to catch Sherrod on MSNBC, Peter? | [](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffd73/4462dc26/8b3b85fe/201f0801/3317219624/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiIzMjY4MTgzIiwiZTN0emJIVm5mWDA9Ijoic2JlMTgxMTE5bSIsImUzdDJaWEp6YVc5dWZYMD0iOiJtIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/sbe181119m?refcode=sbe181119m_m_other&refcode2=3268183")
---
There was also a big piece on Sherrod in the _New York Times_ ...
> _" Rumpled and unvarnished...he has championed populist platitudes like the "dignity of work" that have resonated with working-class voters in all corners of Ohio while also supporting liberal social causes like women's reproductive rights and L.G.B.T.Q rights."_
Whether you swipe a badge or punch a clock, collect tips or collect a salary, Sherrod is on your side. **Let Sherrod know you're on his side, too.**
[**Check out the latest news on Sherrod. Then, if you can, give $5 or more to let him know you're on his side.**](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffd73/4462dc26/8b3b85fe/201f0800/3317219624/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/sbe181119d?refcode=sbe181119d_m")

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: President Trump to California: We have your back
body:
This weekend, President Donald J. Trump visited California to survey damage from the state’s recent deadly wildfires.

https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8ad3dea0ea&e=3ee4985eea
The White House • November 19, 2018

** President Trump to California: We have your back
------------------------------------------------------------
This weekend, President Donald J. Trump visited California to survey damage from the recent deadly wildfires. Meeting with residents whose lives have been forever changed, President Trump’s message was clear: We are with you.

During times like these, our Nation’s first responders take on the toughest jobs. While on the ground in Malibu, California, President Trump took time to thank these heroes for their service. “The men and women that are fighting this fire are incredible,” the President said.

The Administration wasted no time responding to the crisis. On November 9, the President issued an emergency declaration (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=01a77ff7ea&e=3ee4985eea) for the State of California, ordering Federal assistance to supplement State, local, and tribal response efforts. The Federal-wide response has included the U.S. Department of Agriculture’s Forest Service, the Department of Health and Human Services, the Department of Interior, U.S. Small Business Administration, and the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA).

“California Gov. Jerry Brown (D) said Sunday that President Trump [told] him that he has California's back,” Megan Keller reported for The Hill. “The president not only has signed a presidential declaration giving California substantial funding, but he said and pledged very specifically to — to continue to help us," Gov. Brown said.

Watch President Trump survey damage from the devastating wildfires. (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=4079a1e5e5&e=3ee4985eea)

More: How the Trump Administration is providing needed support (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9e572637cf&e=3ee4985eea)

** It's time to vote . . . in the 2018 turkey pardon!
------------------------------------------------------------
Tomorrow, President Trump will pardon the National Thanksgiving Turkey at a ceremony in the Rose Garden, continuing a longstanding White House tradition.

It’s up to you to decide which bird he pardons. Our two feathered candidates—Peas and Carrots—arrived in Washington over the weekend. Be sure to cast your vote before polls close at 11 a.m. ET tomorrow, November 20.

Vote now and make your voices heard! (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=ffc7fde184&e=3ee4985eea)

** America’s highest civilian honor
------------------------------------------------------------
On Friday, President Trump presented seven incredible Americans with the Presidential Medal of Freedom, an honor reserved for those who have made outstanding contributions to the security or national interests of America, to world peace, or to cultural or other significant public or private endeavors.

This year’s recipients certainly fit that bill: Sen. Orrin Hatch, the late Justice Antonin Scalia, Miriam Adelson, Roger Staubach, Alan Page, and two other American legends who are no longer with us—Babe Ruth and Elvis Presley.

See more of these 7 extraordinary American stories. (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=083e81bf4d&e=3ee4985eea)

** Photo of the Day
------------------------------------------------------------
Official White House Photo by Alexi De La Cruz
This year’s candidates for National Thanksgiving Turkey, Peas and Carrots, settle into their room at The Willard Hotel | November 18, 2018

Privacy Policy (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9c1146bf31&e=3ee4985eea) | Contact the White House (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=399a47e15c&e=3ee4985eea) | Unsubscribe (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=3e14714507)

============================================================
The White House . 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW . Washington, DC 20500 . USA · 202-456-1111

name: Jason Yates
subject: Big day coming up!
body:
| | A special video message.
---
[ ](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66idPwV7aJGl24pjLpVkd1GfdQupMvzYbEy60fviRYsuRw6gQvuPgiOJk_P0UymDa6YA&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181120com01&n=1)
| Peter, The elections are over, Thanksgiving is right around the corner and the Christmas preparations are well underway! And the most important day following Black Friday and Cyber Monday is #GivingTuesday – just one week from today! [](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=6_nuz-gPbh4AR2HufX66idPwV7aJGl24pjLpVkd1GfdQupMvzYbEy60fviRYsuRw6gQvuPgiOJk_P0UymDa6YA&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181120com01&n=2) Watch my brief video update message and I hope you will join us on #GivingTuesday to continue making a difference in our nation! **United. We Stand.** Jason Yates
CEO
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181120com01&n=3)

name: Ben Jealous
subject: She said she'd be front row for this
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
body:
We have to stand with Mike Espy.

| [](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c3790f8/7ab0e2c4/7e729aae/3499abfd/2601234086/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-espy?refcode=OHQ11208A") Friend -- We have one week left until the final test of 2018 -- and we could make history one more time. There's one big race left this year: Mike Espy is running to be the first African-American senator from Mississippi since Reconstruction. He's close -- he and his opponent were nearly tied in the election earlier this month, and now it's headed to a runoff. But last week, his opponent, who is a sitting U.S. senator, decided to joke about a "public hanging." Her exact words were: "If [Colin Hutchinson] invited me to a public hanging, I'd be in the front row." **This comment proves that she has no knowledge about or concern for the experience of Mississippians who don't look like her. And it's why we need to elect Mike Espy to the U.S. Senate.** [**Donate $5 or more now to make sure we don't send yet another race-baiting Republican to Washington to do Donald Trump's bidding.**](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c3790f8/7ab0e2c4/7e729aae/3499abfd/2601234086/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-espy?refcode=OHQ11208A") _Or, if your payment information is saved with ActBlue, click below and your contribution will go through immediately:_ | | [**$5 »**](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c3790f8/7ab0e2c4/7e729aae/3499abfc/2601234086/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-espy?refcode=OHQ11208A&amount=5&express_lane=true")
---
| | [**$25 »**](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c3790f8/7ab0e2c4/7e729aae/3499abff/2601234086/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-espy?refcode=OHQ11208A&amount=25&express_lane=true")
---
| [**$50 »**](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c3790f8/7ab0e2c4/7e729aae/3499abfe/2601234086/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-espy?refcode=OHQ11208A&amount=50&express_lane=true")
---
| | [**$100 »**](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c3790f8/7ab0e2c4/7e729aae/3499abf9/2601234086/VEsA/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-espy?refcode=OHQ11208A&amount=100&express_lane=true")
---
| [**$250 »**](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c3790f8/7ab0e2c4/7e729aae/3499abf8/2601234086/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-espy?refcode=OHQ11208A&amount=250&express_lane=true")
---
| | [**OTHER »**](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c3790f8/7ab0e2c4/7e729aae/3499abfb/2601234086/VEsO/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-espy?refcode=OHQ11208A&express_lane=true")
---
Mississippi's dark past is nothing to joke about. In the time between the Civil War and the Civil Rights Era, 650 murders took place, the most of any state in the nation. The stories of these lynchings are truly horrific, and any joke about them is nothing short of despicable.
But not only that, this same sitting Republican senator "joked" that Mississippi might want to make it more difficult for "liberal folks" to vote. We saw what happened in Florida and Georgia when "liberal folks" were targeted at their polling places. Much like "public hanging" is code for lynching, "liberal folks" is code for people of color. These comments are insidious, and those who make them should have no place in the U.S. Senate.
That is why we need to support Mike Espy today. Mike is an experienced leader who I know will bring Mississippi together. He knows how to get things done and stand up for ALL the people of Mississippi, which his opponent has shown she clearly can't or won't be able to do.
[**Let's send Mike Espy to the U.S. Senate -- donate $5 or more now to help him win his runoff election in Mississippi next week.**](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c3790f8/7ab0e2c4/7e729aae/3499abfd/2601234086/VEsP/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-espy?refcode=OHQ11208A")
Thanks,
Ben

[CHIP IN »](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c3790f8/7ab0e2c4/7e729aae/3499abfd/2601234086/VEsHBQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-espy?refcode=OHQ11208A")
---

* * *
PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
---

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is one of the most critical tools for our campaign, but you can let us know if you would like to [receive fewer emails](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c3790f8/7ab0e2c4/7e729aae/3499abe5/2601234086/VEsHBw/ "http://act.benjealous.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails?source=emfooter") going forward. If you'd like to [unsubscribe](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c3790f8/7ab0e2c4/7e729aae/3499abe4/2601234086/VEsHBg/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "http://act.benjealous.com/page/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com"), you can do so here. [Make a contribution to Ben and Susie here](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c3790f8/7ab0e2c4/7e729aae/3499abe7/2601234086/VEsHAQ/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bjst_contribute?refcode=emfooter"). Contributions or gifts to Friends of Ben Jealous are not tax deductible.

name: Maggie Hassan
subject: Mississippi - another chance to win a Senate seat this month
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Hi there,
In just one week, voters in Mississippi will return to the polls for a runoff election for U.S. Senate. The exciting news is that Democrat Mike Espy has a real shot at victory.
I'll be honest with you -- I don't just want Mike to win, we really need him to. You see, after losing some pretty important Senate seats held by Democrats earlier this month, bringing home a win in Mississippi would really help us stand strong against the worst parts of President Trump's agenda.
Mike can't win without your support, everyone, so I have to ask:
[**Please rush $3 or as much as you 're able to support Mike today so that he can build the strongest campaign possible.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc11c/2a380d31/3445866c/f1775a/3376111939/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiMjAxOF9td2g0ZXNweS1ydW5vZmYiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDE4MTEyMF9NV0g0RXNweVJ1bk9mZl82bW8iLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiMywyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/2018_mwh4espy-runoff?refcode=e20181120_MWH4EspyRunOff_6mo&amounts=3,25,50,100,250&recurring=auto")
This is likely our best shot at winning another Senate seat before 2020, so we have to fight until the very end. When Democrats win, it's because we pour our heart and soul into a race -- even tough ones like this.
[**The only way Mike can win in Mississippi is with your help, everyone, so please consider chipping in $3 or however much you can right now. There is still time to make a difference, but it 's running out.**](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc11c/2a380d31/3445866c/f1775b/3376111939/VEsE/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiMjAxOF9td2g0ZXNweS1ydW5vZmYiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDE4MTEyMF9NV0g0RXNweVJ1bk9mZl82bW8iLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiMywyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwIn0=/ "http://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/2018_mwh4espy-runoff?refcode=e20181120_MWH4EspyRunOff_6mo&amounts=3,25,50,100,250&recurring=auto")
With every good wish,
Maggie Hassan

[Contribute](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc11c/2a380d31/3445866c/f17744/3376111939/VEsF/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiMjAxOF9td2g0ZXNweS1ydW5vZmYiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDE4MTEyMF9NV0g0RXNweVJ1bk9mZl82bW8iLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiMywyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/2018_mwh4espy-runoff?refcode=e20181120_MWH4EspyRunOff_6mo&amounts=3,25,50,100,250")

_ _
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
To unsubscribe from the Maggie for NH email list, please click here: [Unsubscribe](http://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc11c/2a380d31/3445866c/f17745/3376111939/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSJ9/ "http://act.maggiehassan.com/unsubscribe?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com")

clean_body: Hi there, In just one week, voters in Mississippi will return to the polls for a runoff election for U.S. Senate. The exciting news is that Democrat Mike Espy has a real shot at victory. I'll be honest with you -- I don't just want Mike to win, we really need him to. You see, after losing some pretty important Senate seats held by Democrats earlier this month, bringing home a win in Mississippi would really help us stand strong against the worst parts of President Trump's agenda. Mike can't win without your support, everyone, so I have to ask: "" This is likely our best shot at winning another Senate seat before 2020, so we have to fight until the very end. When Democrats win, it's because we pour our heart and soul into a race -- even tough ones like this. "" With every good wish, Maggie Hassan "" _

name: Senator Chris Van Hollen
subject: Senate Update: Giving Thanks and Looking Ahead
body:
| | | [Click here to open this e-mail in its own browser window](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?link=SenVanHollen%2501136821%2501November%2bNewsletter%250115442.12276719.4100659.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501) | [Click here to open a plain text version of this email](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?plain_text=Y&link=SenVanHollen%2501136821%2501November%2bNewsletter%250115442.12276719.4100659.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501)
---|---
| | | **November 20, 2018** ** ** |
---|---|---|---
| | | As we head into Thanksgiving, I'm reminded of all the reasons we have to be thankful. At a time when our country faces challenges on many fronts, it is important to also remember the progress we've made and all the good that can be done. For example, I recently visited Baltimore Corps - a local nonprofit that seeks to connect talented young individuals trying to make a difference with great organizations around Baltimore. I had the opportunity to listen to some of the participants and hear their passion and ideas for improving our community. Also, just last week, I attended Montgomery County's Adoption Day Ceremony and joined eight families in celebrating the joy of providing a child with a loving and stable home. I was also honored to meet with a young woman that my team was able to help during her adoption process. In our office, we advocate non-stop for Maryland families - whether that's helping them navigate federal agencies or fighting for access to affordable health care and important patient protections. I am grateful for all of you who have stood with us in this important work, and I hope you'll continue to speak out and fight to make our nation a more perfect union. This week is also a chance to take some time with family and friends. I'm fortunate to have the opportunity to spend my Thanksgiving at home with my family, and I hope you're able to spend this time with your loved ones as well. From our family to yours - Happy Thanksgiving! Chris Van Hollen
---
### **What 's Next**
Every two years, Americans head to the polls to exercise their right to vote. I salute everyone who made their voice heard and participated in our great democratic process - it's something we should never take for granted. As elections come and go, my commitment to you and to Maryland remains the same. Together, we will continue to fight for a better future for our children, an economy that works for everyone, a system that truly produces liberty and justice for all, and a healthy environment and planet. The main order of business for Congress before the end of the year is to ensure that federal agencies have the resources they need to continue providing vital services to the American people. While the President has threatened to trigger a government shutdown, I believe there is bipartisan opposition to allowing that to happen. The good news is that we've already secured important Maryland and national priorities, including investments to continue major water infrastructure projects, address the maintenance backlog at Veterans' hospitals and clinics, further life-saving medical research, and provide more resources to fight the opioid epidemic. As a member of the Senate Appropriations Committee, I will keep fighting for programs that are crucial to our state and our country - whether that's to protect the Chesapeake Bay, strengthen our transportation systems, or invest in law enforcement and implement criminal justice reforms. I'm also committed to protecting the Affordable Care Act, Medicare, Medicaid, and Social Security. Any efforts to undermine these vital programs - especially under the guise of deficit reduction after Washington Republicans passed $2 trillion in tax breaks that disproportionately help the very wealthy - will be met with fierce opposition.
### **Protecting the Integrity of the Department of Justice**
The Attorney General must be a champion for the people - but it's clear the President wants someone who pledges loyalty to him, not the Constitution. That's why President Trump fired Jeff Sessions and tapped Matthew Whitaker, who has repeatedly demonstrated his bias against the independent Mueller investigation, to temporarily fill the spot. To date, despite his obvious conflicts, Whitaker has refused to recuse himself from overseeing that investigation. While the legal challenges to the constitutionality of Whitaker's appointment proceed, it is essential that the Senate pass bipartisan legislation to protect the integrity of the Special Counsel's investigation into Russian interference and obstruction of justice. But last week, Republican Senate Leader Mitch McConnell took to the Senate floor and blocked a vote on this bill - a shameful decision. We must come together as patriots, not partisans, to protect our democracy - and I'll keep fighting for this legislation.
### **Advanced Manufacturing in Maryland**
#
Recently, I participated in the launch of an exciting new program that has huge potential for Maryland and the country. The Advanced Manufacturing, Materials, and Processes (AMMP) project - based in Harford County - is a new program to advance and build breakthrough, innovative manufacturing technologies. From upgrading 3-D printing to creating entirely new technologies, this program will work with the Army Research Laboratory, local businesses, and Maryland's universities to revolutionize our manufacturing industry. AMMP will create jobs here in Maryland and drive new economic growth. What's more, the research and technology developed here will have limitless possibilities - from modernizing American manufacturing to better preparing our troops on the frontlines. I was proud to help secure significant funding for this crucial project through my role on the Senate Appropriations Committee, and I will continue working to bring good-paying jobs and new opportunities to Maryland.
[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15442.12276719.4100659.7517223&report_id=Facebook&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.facebook.com%2fchrisvanhollen%2f&redir_log=328155479639284)[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15442.12276719.4100659.7517223&report_id=Twitter&redirect=https%3a%2f%2ftwitter.com%2fChrisVanHollen&redir_log=957640916473241)[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15442.12276719.4100659.7517223&report_id=Instagram&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.instagram.com%2fchrisvanhollen%2f&redir_log=389295287187292)[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15442.12276719.4100659.7517223&report_id=YouTube&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fuser%2fRepChrisVanHollen&redir_log=463627374976326)[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15442.12276719.4100659.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fmedium.com%2f%40ChrisVanHollen&redir_log=575666539451239)
|
---
[Please click here to unsubscribe](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//EsurveyForm.aspx?__cid=SenVanHollen&__sid=100007&__crop=15442.12276719.4100659.7517223)

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: Today, President Trump will pardon the National Thanksgiving Turkey
body:
The midterms elections may have ended earlier this month but another election season is in full flight | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c5b4900ca7&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Feast Your Eyes on the Two Turkeys Appealing for Presidential Pardon**

“The midterms elections may have ended earlier this month but another election season is in full flight between the two turkeys vying to become this year’s official National Thanksgiving Turkey. This year’s candidates: Peas and Carrots,” Jordyn Phelps reports for ABC News.

“One of the two turkeys will be selected via an online White House poll to become this year's National Thanksgiving Turkey. The turkeys have been carefully groomed for a White House event, trained from a young age to be comfortable with lights, crowds, and music to make them comfortable in the limelight.”

[Click here to continue reading.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=910b74b38f&e=3ee4985eea)

**Watch live at 1:05 p.m. ET:** [President Trump pardons the National Thanksgiving Turkey](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e523331d9a&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “It's time to deck the halls: The holiday season officially kicked off at the White House Monday when President Donald Trump and first lady Melania Trump received the 2018 White House Christmas tree,” Betsy Klein reports for [CNN](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=baa73d73fb&e=3ee4985eea). “The tree arrived at the North Portico of the White House by jingle bell-adorned horse and carriage, continuing an annual tradition.”
---
|
---
| “A remarkably low 13 percent are calling President Trump’s economy a problem as confidence is hitting a 14-year high, and seven in 10 said 2018 is a good time to find a quality job,” Paul Bedard reports for the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6a6d9e69f2&e=3ee4985eea)_. The new Gallup survey shows that economic confidence for November remains at its highest since 2004. “It has been nearly two decades since Americans have been as positive about the U.S. economy as they are now,” Gallup says.
---
|
---
| “President Donald Trump is succeeding in making China pay most of the cost of his trade war,” Xiaoqing Pi writes for [Bloomberg](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=66155a3456&e=3ee4985eea). “Through its strategic choice of Chinese products, the U.S. government was not only able to minimize the negative effects on U.S. consumers and firms, but also to create substantial net welfare gains in the U.S.,” a new research paper from EconPol Europe says.
---
|
---
| “As many as 10,000 migrants are believed to be traveling with caravans from Central America to the United States with the hopes of entering the country, senior administration officials said Monday,” Anna Giaritelli writes for the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=eb2dc84d6a&e=3ee4985eea)_. “Between 8,500 and 10,500 people are making their way from Northern Triangle countries El Salvador, Guatemala, and Honduras to America’s southern border. About 6,000 have already made it to Tijuana, a city of 1.7 million residents that sits just over the border from San Diego, Calif.”
---
|

[Privacy Policy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e84727a8b7&e=3ee4985eea) | [Contact the White House](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7f709259aa&e=3ee4985eea) | [Unsubscribe](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=4f12d8e829)
---
| | | The White House * 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW * Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111
---

name: Stacey Abrams
subject: Lindy Miller needs our help before her Dec. 4 runoff election.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stacey Abrams for Governor
body:
Lindy Miller needs our help in her runoff campaign for Public Service Commissioner. |
---
[](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa669/34a064cc/5daee7e/70c0070/3976431287/VEsH/ "https://staceyabrams.com/")

| | | Friend: I'm writing to you today on behalf of my friend, Lindy Miller, who needs our help in her run for Public Service Commissioner. Public Service Commissioners are in charge of regulating public utilities like energy. Having a strong Commissioner like Lindy would allow us to make energy bills affordable for Georgia families while still protecting our natural resources. Lindy is a lifelong Georgian with over a decade of experience as a businesswoman and small business owner. She understands that building a brighter future for Georgia requires investing in our infrastructure and bringing new, clean-energy jobs to our state. Lindy Miller has already pushed a 12-year Republican incumbent into a runoff election. That's no easy feat. But her race is extremely close, so she needs all of us standing beside her if she's going to win on December 4th. That's why I ask: **[Will you make a donation to Lindy Miller's runoff campaign for Public Service Commissioner?](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa669/34a064cc/5daee7e/70c0071/3976431287/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiSUUxMTIwMjAxOC1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sya-miller?refcode=IE11202018-full")** With the right priorities, we can make energy affordable, reliable, and clean for all Georgians. But in order to get there, we need Lindy Miller leading the charge. I hope she can count on your support. Sincerely, Stacey Abrams
---

**[DONATE](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa669/34a064cc/5daee7e/70c0071/3976431287/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiSUUxMTIwMjAxOC1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sya-miller?refcode=IE11202018-full")**

|
---
[](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa669/34a064cc/5daee7e/70c007e/3976431287/VEsC/ "https://www.facebook.com/stacey.abrams.77/") [](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa669/34a064cc/5daee7e/70c007f/3976431287/VEsD/ "https://twitter.com/staceyabrams") [](https://secure.staceyabrams.com/page/m/41efa669/34a064cc/5daee7e/70c007c/3976431287/VEsA/ "https://www.instagram.com/staceyabrams/")

Suite D120-447
Atlanta, GA 30307

name: Abigail Spanberger
subject: What do you think, Peter?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Congress
body:
It's been two weeks, and I am still in awe of this team of supporters and overwhelmed with gratitude for the Virginians who placed their trust in me to represent them in Congress. And these two weeks have been very busy, because our work is only just beginning.
| | Peter, It's been two weeks, and I am still in awe of this team of supporters and overwhelmed with gratitude for the Virginians who placed their trust in me to represent them in Congress. **And these two weeks have been very busy, Peter, because our work is only just beginning.** Since Election Night, I've heard from families across our district about their hopes for our community and what we can do together to make it better. I've sat down with reporters in Central Virginia to discuss our campaign and our next steps. And last week, I spent some time in Washington, D.C., for new member orientation. My fellow representatives-elect and I toured the U.S. Capitol for the first time and we posed for our freshman class picture. I even got set up with an ID badge -- my husband Adam joked that it was like I was going off to college. Now, my team is preparing for the busy two years ahead of us, and I want to hear from you as we set our priorities. You elected me to be an accountable, accessible leader for our community, and I'm committed to listening to you every step of the way. [**Take our survey to tell me what you 'd like the next Congress to prioritize, Peter.**](https://act.myngp.com/el/p99GtbkxGTpjMnNJ6uWq2o34xb39Qnga3Ey-lYKKXr8=/MbiI3jBJ6GdBiaIafr-EEzMVssoJG_iRzItGlQsRDp8=) I sincerely appreciate the feedback! Thank you, and I wish you and your families a relaxing and happy Thanksgiving! \- Abigail [](https://act.myngp.com/el/p99GtbkxGTpjMnNJ6uWq2o34xb39Qnga3Ey-lYKKXr8=/lNivoirz2JpbqI0ApAVxzwkgO8PQpIUPxTJywB6JoMM=?refcode=em_pet_2018.11.20_b1_as_all&amount=15&amounts=15,25,50,100,250,500,1000) [](https://act.myngp.com/el/p99GtbkxGTpjMnNJ6uWq2o34xb39Qnga3Ey-lYKKXr8=/ULOQwY0Xf4N_Mj9wbG9MUl0J5bklT98YExW5D6bS4gM=?refcode=footer&amount=15&amounts=15,25,50,100,250,500,1000) _Abigail Spanberger is a proud Democrat, former CIA officer, and representative-elect for Virginia 's 7th Congressional District. She believes in leadership that flows from our shared American values of integrity, responsibility, and decency, and she wants to work to move this country forward together. To learn more, please visit [AbigailSpanberger.com](https://act.myngp.com/el/p99GtbkxGTpjMnNJ6uWq2o34xb39Qnga3Ey-lYKKXr8=/PL4WFLGMTRN800EEPlxiTR2MOszRMPXwGuzkx7IlCLM=)_
| Paid for by Spanberger for Congress
---
Spanberger for Congress
PO Box 3121
Glen Allen VA 23058 United States
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please [unsubscribe](https://act.myngp.com/el/p99GtbkxGTpjMnNJ6uWq2o34xb39Qnga3Ey-lYKKXr8=/hs4XJAcBRapTmd_fniwd1k7hsWsJYU80eBLYhB_Voc8=).

My fellow representatives-elect and I toured the U.S. Capitol for the first time and we posed for our freshman class picture. I even got set up with an ID badge -- my husband Adam joked that it was like I was going off to college. Now, my team is preparing for the busy two years ahead of us, and I want to hear from you as we set our priorities. You elected me to be an accountable, accessible leader for our community, and I'm committed to listening to you every step of the way. = I sincerely appreciate the feedback! Thank you, and I wish you and your families a relaxing and happy Thanksgiving! \- Abigail Abigail Spanberger is a proud Democrat, former CIA officer, and representative-elect for Virginia 's 7th Congressional District. She believes in leadership that flows from our shared American values of integrity, responsibility, and decency, and she wants to work to move this country forward together. To learn more, please visit = | Paid for by Spanberger for Congress Spanberger for Congress PO Box 3121 Glen Allen VA 23058 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please =.

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20White=20House?=
subject: President Trump crowns the 2018 National Thanksgiving Turkey
body:
Today in the Rose Garden, President Donald J. Trump participated in a classic Thanksgiving tradition: the White House turkey pardoning.

https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c9b9a837fc&e=3ee4985eea
The White House • November 20, 2018

** President Trump crowns the 2018 National Thanksgiving Turkey
------------------------------------------------------------
Today in the Rose Garden, President Donald J. Trump participated in a classic Thanksgiving tradition: the White House turkey pardoning. From the moment polls opened yesterday, Americans began casting their votes online for our two very qualified candidates, Peas and Carrots.

With the votes now tallied, by a razor-thin margin, Peas has earned the honor of 2018 National Thanksgiving Turkey!

Along with his runner-up and alternate, Carrots, Peas will move to the “Gobbler’s Rest” exhibit in Blacksburg, Virginia. At Gobbler’s Rest, students and veterinarians within Virginia Tech’s Department of Animal and Poultry Sciences will care for both turkeys, who will soon be available for public visitation.

“As we gather together this week with those we love, we share our gratitude to all of those who spend this holiday very, very far from home, serving in our military overseas,” President Trump said from the Rose Garden. “We send our eternal gratitude to the heroes who keep America safe, strong, proud, prosperous, and free.”

Watch Peas be crowned as this year’s National Thanksgiving Turkey! (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2b756f273d&e=3ee4985eea)

Something to share: President Trump on the history of the turkey pardon (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6dac9f9bd9&e=3ee4985eea)

** Vice President Pence returns from Asia
------------------------------------------------------------
Vice President Mike Pence visited Japan, Singapore, Australia, and Papua New Guinea in the past week to reaffirm President Trump’s commitment to a free and open Indo-Pacific—one where all nations are sovereign, strong, and prosperous.

In Papua New Guinea, the Vice President attended the Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation (APEC) Summit, highlighting American leadership in promoting fair and reciprocal trade. “While we’ve been forging new deals, we’ve also stood up to countries that use unfair trade practices. Just look at the stand that President Trump has taken on our trading relationship with China,” the Vice President said.

“We hope for better. The United States though will not change course until China changes its ways.”

Watch Vice President Pence at the APEC Summit in Papua New Guinea (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=a42275da35&e=3ee4985eea)

Go deeper: The Vice President’s full remarks to APEC (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=2bd926a947&e=3ee4985eea)

** Photo of the Day
------------------------------------------------------------
Official White House Photo by Andrea Hanks
Peas, the 2018 National Thanksgiving Turkey, prepares to be pardoned by President Donald J. Trump | November 20, 2018

Privacy Policy (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=3fdd4caa14&e=3ee4985eea) | Contact the White House (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=59a902f822&e=3ee4985eea) | Unsubscribe (https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=8cafb01033&e=3ee4985eea&c=f7bec55a4c)

============================================================
The White House . 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW . Washington, DC 20500 . USA · 202-456-1111
